mapping.a_element: conformally invariant scaling limit of 2D lattice models (e.g., percolation, Ising model)
mapping.b_element: conformally invariant scaling limit of loop-erased random walk and uniform spanning tree

mapping.a_element: Schramm-Loewner Evolution (SLE) as the governing conformally invariant process
mapping.b_element: SLE(2) and SLE(8) as the scaling limits of loop-erased random walk and uniform spanning tree Peano curve, respectively

mapping.a_element: conjectured conformal invariance of scaling exponents and dimensions in lattice models
mapping.b_element: proven conformal invariance of the scaling limit of loop-erased random walk and uniform spanning tree

mapping.a_element: lattice models with conformally invariant scaling limits
mapping.b_element: loop-erased random walk and uniform spanning tree as specific lattice models with conformally invariant scaling limits

brief_justification: Both papers address the conformal invariance of scaling limits in 2D lattice models, with Schramm-Loewner Evolution (SLE) serving as the underlying mathematical machinery. Paper A discusses the conjecture and framework for proving conformal invariance, while Paper B provides rigorous proofs for specific models (loop-erased random walk and uniform spanning trees) using SLE as the conformally invariant process.
